title: Ulgo
content:

{{Quote|Back, hairy vermin, before I have you made into a foot wipe.|translated=1|Ulgo threatens the warriors of Bright Tree Village|<ref name="TAOT" />}}
'''Ulgo''', who claimed to hold the title '''King Ulgo the Magnificent''', was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Dulok/Legends|Dulok]] from the [[Endor/Legends|forest moon]] of [[Endor (planet)/Legends|Endor]], where he lived in a cave near the [[Canyons of Mist]]. There he was confronted by a group of [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] from [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]], who had been misled by another Dulok named [[Vulgarr/Legends|Vulgarr]] to believe a monster lived in Ulgo's home. When the Ewoks rescued a group of [[Lantern bird/Legends|lantern birds]] that Ulgo planned to eat, he attempted to attack them but fell to his [[Death/Legends|death]] in the ensuing struggle.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Slay him! He is evil. He turns the skin of our people into a filthy cloak.|translated=1|Pondo is angered by Ulgo's vest|<ref name="TAOT" />}}
The [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Dulok/Legends|Dulok]] Ulgo lived on the [[Endor/Legends|forest moon]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Endor (planet)/Legends|Endor]], [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of his [[Species/Legends|species]]. He claimed to be [[King/Legends|king]] of all Duloks,<ref name="TAOT" /> but lived alone near<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|278|Ulgo}}</ref> the [[Canyons of Mist]] in a cave high in the canyon walls near the [[Whispering River]]. There, he set snares made from [[stranglevine]] to capture [[Lantern bird/Legends|lantern birds]], which he both ate and used as a light source within his dark cave. Ulgo at one point interacted with [[Vulgarr/Legends|Vulgarr]], another Dulok who claimed the title of king, and traded him a pair of lantern [[Bird/Legends|birds]] in exchange for a vest made from the furs of [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]], another [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] species who called the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] home. Vulgarr later made plans to capture the [[Wokling/Legends|Woklings]] of an Ewok settlement<ref name="TAOT" /> named [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]],<ref name="EGTC">''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> and in order to draw the village's warriors away first captured a single wokling named [[Malani]]. Vulgarr then visited the Ewoks and claimed a great monster had taken the child, directing them to Ulgo's home, which he claimed was the monster's lair.<ref name="TAOT" /> 

[[File:Warok-TAFA.png|thumb|left|Ulgo was killed after attacking the Ewok Warok.|200px]]
The warriors of Bright Tree Village followed the Whispering River to Ulgo's home using [[Hang-glider/Legends|gliders]], where Ulgo confronted them and order them to leave his land. The warriors' leader, [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]], explained that Vulgarr had misled them, causing Ulgo to show off the Ewok fur vest the other king had traded him. This upset the Ewoks, especially the warrior [[Pondo]], who attempted to attack the Dulok before being constrained by his colleagues. Ulgo reacted to the attempted attack by threatening the Ewoks, who made to leave.

Before departing, however, [[Warok/Legends|Warok]], the [[Parent/Legends|father]] of Malani, took the caged lantern birds Ulgo was carrying and informed him that the creatures were servants of the [[Great Spirit]] of the forest and must be set free. Enraged at losing his breakfast, Ulgo lunged at the Ewok, who side stepped the attack and accidentally knocked the Dulok with his glider, sending the king tumbling off a cliff into the canyon below. At first the Ewoks believed Ulgo would survive the fall by landing in deep water, but a strong gust of wind changed the falling Dulok's path and caused him to land on a large flat rock, which [[Death/Legends|killed]] him. Chirpa then whispered an oath for Ulgo, as the Ewoks did for any forest creature who died.<ref name="TAOT" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|A Dulok? No! Not a Dulok! The king of all Duloks.|translated=1|Ulgo claimed to be king of the Duloks|<ref name="TAOT" />}}
Ulgo lived alone in the Canyons of Mists, but nevertheless, claimed to be king of all Duloks. He considered Vulgarr, another Dulok king, to be a fool because he was willing to trade a fine Ewok fur vest for two lantern birds. When angered by the warrior of Bright Tree Village, the Dulok hissed and spit at them, threatening to use their fur to make foot wipes. An elderly, bent and wizened Dulok, Ulgo had mangy fur and spoke in a deep, growling voice.<ref name="TAOT" />

==Equipment==
{{Quote|Vulgarr is a moon-headed fool! I once traded him two scrawny birds for my fine fur vest.|Ulgo becomes angry after Warok takes his lantern birds|<ref name="TAOT" />}}
At the time of his meeting with the warriors of Bright Tree Village, Ulgo carried a long-handled [[flint]] [[Axe/Legends|axe]] and wore a fine Ewok fur vest. He used a small portable wooden cage to contain captured lantern birds, which he used as a light source.<ref name="TAOT" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ulgo was created for the children's novel ''[[The Adventures of Teebo: A Tale of Magic and Suspense]]'', which was written by [[Joe Johnston]] and released in [[1984]].

==Appearances==
*''[[The Adventures of Teebo: A Tale of Magic and Suspense]]'' {{1st}}
